title: Code Rules for Python 3.13+
description: Machine-readable rules for agents. ALL rules are mandatory. CODE-PATTERNS.md contains design guidance and extended examples.

mypy.note: For existing codebases, enable strict mode incrementally using per-module overrides in pyproject.toml

ANTI-001.name: Mutable Default Arguments
ANTI-001.description: Mutable default arguments are shared across calls, causing unexpected behavior.
ANTI-001.ifThen: IF function has mutable default THEN use None and initialize inside
ANTI-001.correctPattern: def process_files(files: list | None = None): files = files or []

ANTI-003.name: Global Mutable State
ANTI-003.description: Global state causes unpredictable behavior and makes testing difficult.
ANTI-003.ifThen: IF need shared state THEN use class attributes or dependency injection

ANTI-004.name: Fire-and-Forget Async Tasks
ANTI-004.description: Fire-and-forget tasks can be garbage collected, causing silent failures.
ANTI-004.ifThen: IF creating background task THEN store reference and handle completion

ANTI-005.name: Using type() for Type Checking
ANTI-005.description: type() checks break inheritance; isinstance handles subclasses.
ANTI-005.ifThen: IF checking type THEN use isinstance()

ANTI-006.name: Complex Lambdas
ANTI-006.description: Complex lambdas are hard to debug and have poor stack traces.
ANTI-006.ifThen: IF lambda has complex logic THEN convert to named function
ANTI-006.detectHeuristic: Lambda with multiple method calls or conditionals
ANTI-006.correctPattern: def normalize_string(x: str) -> str: return x.strip().lower().replace('-', '_') if x else ''
ANTI-006.antiPattern: processor = lambda x: x.strip().lower().replace('-', '_') if x else ''

ANTI-007.name: String Concatenation in Loops
ANTI-007.description: String concatenation in loops is O(n²) complexity.
ANTI-007.ifThen: IF building string in loop THEN use ''.join()
ANTI-007.antiPattern: result = ''; for line in lines: result += line

ANTI-008.name: Hardcoded Unix Paths
ANTI-008.description: Hardcoded Unix paths break cross-platform compatibility.
ANTI-008.ifThen: IF using home directory THEN use Path.home()

ANTI-009.name: Unhandled JSON Parsing
ANTI-009.ifThen: IF parsing JSON THEN handle JSONDecodeError explicitly
ANTI-009.correctPattern: try: data = json.loads(response) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: raise ParseError(f'Invalid JSON: {e}') from e

ANTI-010.name: Manual Semaphore Acquire/Release
ANTI-010.description: Manual acquire/release can leak semaphore on exceptions.
ANTI-010.ifThen: IF using semaphore THEN use context manager
ANTI-010.antiPattern: await semaphore.acquire(); try: await work() finally: semaphore.release()

ARCH-001.name: Separate Decision Logic from Infrastructure
ARCH-001.description: A function should not mix business logic (decisions) with infrastructure (I/O). Separate pure decision logic from I/O operations.
ARCH-001.ifThen: IF function has decisions AND I/O THEN split into pure decision function and I/O orchestrator
ARCH-001.detectHeuristic: Function contains both conditionals/validation AND logging/database/HTTP/filesystem
ARCH-001.correctPattern:
def should_sync(source) -> bool: return source.is_stale()  # Pure decision
def sync_source(source): logger.info(...); if not should_sync(source): return ...; data = fetch_from_remote(...)  # I/O orchestration
ARCH-001.antiPattern: def sync_source(source): logger.info(...); if source.is_stale(): data = fetch_from_remote(...); logger.info(...)

ARCH-002.name: Inject Time as a Parameter
ARCH-002.description: Never call datetime.now() or time.time() inside business logic. Pass time as a parameter for testability.
ARCH-002.ifThen: IF function needs current time THEN accept time as parameter
ARCH-002.detectHeuristic: datetime.now() or time.time() in function with business logic
ARCH-002.correctPattern: def is_token_expired(token, now: datetime) -> bool: return token.expires_at < now

ARCH-003.name: Return Results for Logging, Don't Log Inline
ARCH-003.description: Pure functions should return information that enables logging, not perform it. Return metadata, let caller log.
ARCH-003.ifThen: IF function has business logic AND logging THEN return result object with metadata, log at boundary
ARCH-003.detectHeuristic: logger.* call inside function that also has business logic
ARCH-003.correctPattern: def process_items(items) -> ProcessingResult: return ProcessingResult(results=results, skipped=skipped)  # Caller logs

ARCH-004.name: Return Decisions, Don't Execute Them
ARCH-004.description: For complex decision trees, return a decision object; let the caller execute it. Separates classification from action.
ARCH-004.ifThen: IF function decides AND executes THEN split into classifier (returns decision) and executor (acts on decision)
ARCH-004.detectHeuristic: Function with conditionals that call different I/O operations in each branch
ARCH-004.correctPattern:
def classify_payment(payment) -> PaymentAction: ...  # Pure classification
def handle_payment(payment): action = classify_payment(payment); match action.action: ...  # Execute decision
ARCH-004.antiPattern: def handle_payment(payment): if payment.amount > 10000: request_approval(payment) elif ...

ARCH-005.name: Layer Boundaries
ARCH-005.description: Understand which layer you're in. Routes/Handlers: HTTP I/O. Services: orchestrate domain+infra. Domain: pure logic, NO I/O. Repositories: data I/O.
ARCH-005.ifThen: IF writing business logic THEN keep it pure (no I/O); IF need I/O THEN do it in service/adapter layer
ARCH-005.detectHeuristic: Function in domain layer has imports from database/HTTP/logging modules
ARCH-005.antiPattern: Domain function that directly calls database or logs

layerRules.routes: HTTP I/O, request/response mapping, call services
layerRules.domain: Pure logic, NO I/O, NO logging, NO database
layerRules.repositories: Data I/O, SQL/NoSQL queries, external API calls

ASYNC-001.name: Choose TaskGroup or gather based on failure behavior
ASYNC-001.description: TaskGroup cancels all tasks on failure (use for dependent/transactional work). gather with return_exceptions=True continues despite failures (use for independent tasks where partial results are acceptable).
ASYNC-001.ifThen: IF tasks are dependent/transactional THEN TaskGroup; IF tasks are independent and partial results OK THEN gather(return_exceptions=True)
ASYNC-001.correctPattern: TaskGroup for all-or-nothing; gather(return_exceptions=True) for independent tasks
ASYNC-001.antiPattern: Using TaskGroup for independent tasks where partial results are wanted

decisionGuide.gather: Tasks are independent / want partial results (fan-out, batch fetching)
decisionGuide.gatherOrdered: Need ordered results matching input order

ASYNC-002.name: Use asyncio.timeout
ASYNC-002.ifThen: IF need timeout on async operation THEN use asyncio.timeout context

ASYNC-003.name: Only async if contains await
ASYNC-003.description: Functions marked async without await create unnecessary overhead.
ASYNC-003.ifThen: IF function has no await THEN remove async keyword
ASYNC-003.detectHeuristic: async def without await/async for/async with in body

ASYNC-004.name: Always await async calls
ASYNC-004.description: Calling async function without await returns coroutine, not result.
ASYNC-004.ifThen: IF calling async function THEN use await (or pass to TaskGroup/create_task)

ASYNC-005.name: No time.sleep in async
ASYNC-005.ifThen: IF need delay in async THEN use await asyncio.sleep()

ASYNC-006.name: No blocking I/O in async
ASYNC-006.description: Blocking open() in async defeats concurrency.
ASYNC-006.ifThen: IF file I/O in async function THEN use aiofiles
ASYNC-006.correctPattern: async def read(): async with aiofiles.open('f.txt') as f: return await f.read()
ASYNC-006.antiPattern: async def read(): with open('f.txt') as f: return f.read()

CONTROL-001.name: Use match for 3+ branches
CONTROL-001.description: Use pattern matching instead of long if/elif chains.
CONTROL-001.ifThen: IF 3+ conditionals for same variable THEN use match/case
CONTROL-001.antiPattern: if x == 'a': ... elif x == 'b': ... elif x == 'c': ...

DATA-001.name: Use slots and frozen
DATA-001.description: slots=True saves 30-50% memory, frozen=True ensures immutability.
DATA-001.ifThen: IF defining dataclass THEN add slots=True, frozen=True unless mutability needed

DATA-002.name: No mutable defaults
DATA-002.description: Mutable defaults are shared across all instances.
DATA-002.ifThen: IF dataclass field has mutable default THEN use field(default_factory=...)
DATA-002.detectHeuristic: list/dict/set default value in dataclass field

DESIGN-001.name: DRY - Don't Repeat Yourself
DESIGN-001.description: Extract shared logic into reusable functions. If code appears 2+ times, extract it.
DESIGN-001.ifThen: IF same code block appears in 2+ places THEN extract to shared helper
DESIGN-001.detectHeuristic: Look for blocks of 10+ lines that appear multiple times
DESIGN-001.correctPattern: Extract to private helper function with clear name
DESIGN-001.antiPattern: Same validation/logic duplicated across multiple functions

DESIGN-002.name: KISS - Keep It Simple
DESIGN-002.description: Avoid over-engineering. Only add complexity when necessary.
DESIGN-002.ifThen: IF solution seems complex THEN ask 'is there a simpler way?'
DESIGN-002.detectHeuristic: Question abstractions that aren't immediately needed
DESIGN-002.antiPattern: 6+ helper modules when 3 would suffice; premature abstraction

ERROR-001.name: No bare except
ERROR-001.description: Bare except catches KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit.
ERROR-001.ifThen: IF catching exceptions THEN specify exception type

ERROR-002.name: Chain exceptions
ERROR-002.description: from e preserves original exception context.
ERROR-002.ifThen: IF raising in except block THEN add from e
ERROR-002.detectHeuristic: raise in except block without 'from e'
ERROR-002.correctPattern: except ValueError as e: raise ConfigError('Bad config') from e

ERROR-003.name: Don't swallow exceptions
ERROR-003.ifThen: IF catching broad exception THEN log and either re-raise or handle specifically
ERROR-003.detectHeuristic: except Exception followed by pass/return None/continue
ERROR-003.correctPattern: except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError) as e: logger.warning(f'Could not access: {e}'); return None

FASTAPI-001.name: Use Annotated for dependencies
FASTAPI-001.description: Define reusable type aliases with Annotated for FastAPI dependencies.
FASTAPI-001.ifThen: IF using same dependency in multiple endpoints THEN create type alias
FASTAPI-001.detectHeuristic: Same Depends() definition in multiple endpoints

FASTAPI-002.name: Use Protocol types for testability
FASTAPI-002.description: Protocol types allow mocking in tests.
FASTAPI-002.ifThen: IF dependency returns service THEN use Protocol type
FASTAPI-002.detectHeuristic: Concrete class in dependency return type

FILE-001.name: Use pathlib
FILE-001.description: pathlib is more readable and cross-platform.
FILE-001.ifThen: IF doing path operations THEN use pathlib.Path

FILE-002.name: Use context managers
FILE-002.description: Context managers ensure files close on exception.
FILE-002.ifThen: IF opening file THEN use with context manager
FILE-002.correctPattern: with path.open() as f: content = f.read()

INHERIT-001.name: Use @override decorator
INHERIT-001.ifThen: IF method overrides parent/protocol THEN add @override decorator
INHERIT-001.detectHeuristic: Method name matches parent/protocol method without @override
INHERIT-001.antiPattern: def execute(self): # overrides parent without decorator

INHERIT-002.name: Prefer composition over inheritance
INHERIT-002.description: Use composition and protocols instead of deep inheritance.
INHERIT-002.ifThen: IF adding behavior via inheritance THEN consider protocol + composition instead

PYDANTIC-001.name: Use ConfigDict(extra='forbid')
PYDANTIC-001.description: Catches typos and schema violations by rejecting unknown fields.
PYDANTIC-001.ifThen: IF defining Pydantic model THEN add model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid')

PYDANTIC-002.name: Use Field descriptions
PYDANTIC-002.description: Field metadata improves API docs and validation errors.
PYDANTIC-002.ifThen: IF API model field THEN add Field with description and constraints
PYDANTIC-002.detectHeuristic: Pydantic field without Field() with constraints
PYDANTIC-002.correctPattern: limit: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, le=100, description='Max results')

PYDANTIC-003.name: Cross-field validation with model_validator
PYDANTIC-003.description: Use @model_validator for validation that spans multiple fields.
PYDANTIC-003.ifThen: IF validating across fields THEN use @model_validator
PYDANTIC-003.detectHeuristic: Validation logic in __init__ of Pydantic model

RUNTIME-001.name: No assert in production
RUNTIME-001.ifThen: IF validating input THEN use explicit if/raise instead of assert
RUNTIME-001.correctPattern: if not files: raise ValueError('No files provided')

RUNTIME-002.name: No global mutable state
RUNTIME-002.description: Global state causes unpredictable behavior and makes testing difficult.
RUNTIME-002.ifThen: IF need shared state THEN use class attributes, closures, or dependency injection

SECURITY-001.name: No shell=True
SECURITY-001.ifThen: IF using subprocess THEN use shell=False with list of arguments

SHELL-001.name: Errors to stderr
SHELL-001.description: Error messages should go to stderr for proper stream handling.
SHELL-001.ifThen: IF printing error message in shell THEN redirect to stderr

SHELL-002.name: Use set -e
SHELL-002.description: Scripts should exit on error by default.
SHELL-002.ifThen: IF writing shell script THEN add set -e at top
SHELL-002.detectHeuristic: Shell script without set -e near top

SHELL-003.name: Quote variables
SHELL-003.description: Unquoted variables cause word splitting issues.
SHELL-003.ifThen: IF using variable in shell THEN wrap in double quotes

SIZE-001.name: File Size Limit
SIZE-001.description: Files should be < 500 lines. Split large files into focused modules.
SIZE-001.ifThen: IF file > 500 lines THEN identify responsibilities and split into modules
SIZE-001.antiPattern: main.py with 544 lines mixing health, audit, debug endpoints

exemptions.note: Document exemptions with justification in the file header or CLAUDE.md
exemptions.example: MCP server entry points where @mcp.tool() decorators must be co-located with handler logic and tool docstrings

SIZE-002.name: Function Size Limit
SIZE-002.description: Functions should be < 50 lines (100 max). Extract complex logic to helpers.
SIZE-002.ifThen: IF function > 50 lines THEN extract logical sections to helper functions
SIZE-002.correctPattern: Orchestrator function (~20 lines) calling focused helpers
SIZE-002.antiPattern: 112-line function with nested conditionals and mixed concerns

STRING-001.name: Use f-strings
STRING-001.description: f-strings are faster and more readable.
STRING-001.ifThen: IF formatting strings THEN use f-string

STRUCTURE-001.name: One concept per file
STRUCTURE-001.description: Each file should focus on a single concept or closely related set of functions. Split when a file handles multiple unrelated responsibilities or grows beyond 300-500 lines.
STRUCTURE-001.ifThen: IF file handles multiple unrelated concerns THEN split by responsibility into focused modules
STRUCTURE-001.detectHeuristic: File contains classes/functions that change for different reasons, or mixes service/repository/model concerns
STRUCTURE-001.correctPattern: user_service.py (business logic), user_repository.py (data access), user_models.py (data structures)
STRUCTURE-001.antiPattern: user.py containing service, repository, models, and utilities

STRUCTURE-002.name: Define __all__ in package __init__.py
STRUCTURE-002.description: Define the public interface for every package with __all__ in __init__.py. Unlisted members are internal implementation details.
STRUCTURE-002.ifThen: IF package has __init__.py THEN define __all__ listing public exports

STRUCTURE-003.name: Use absolute imports
STRUCTURE-003.description: Use absolute imports for clarity and reliability. Relative imports break when modules are moved or reorganized.
STRUCTURE-003.ifThen: IF importing from project modules THEN use absolute import path
STRUCTURE-003.detectHeuristic: from ..module or from . import in non-test code

TYPE-001.name: Use X | None syntax
TYPE-001.description: Use native union syntax instead of Optional[X] or Union[X, Y].
TYPE-001.ifThen: IF you see Optional[X] THEN replace with X | None

TYPE-002.name: Import from collections.abc
TYPE-002.description: Use collections.abc for Callable, Mapping, Sequence, etc.
TYPE-002.ifThen: IF importing Callable/Mapping/Sequence from typing THEN use collections.abc

TYPE-003.name: Use Self for instance-returning methods
TYPE-003.description: Use Self instead of forward string references.
TYPE-003.ifThen: IF method returns own class instance THEN use Self
TYPE-003.detectHeuristic: Return type is string of own class name

TYPE-004.name: Use type keyword for aliases
TYPE-004.description: Use type keyword for type aliases.
TYPE-004.ifThen: IF defining type alias THEN use type keyword

TYPE-005.name: Avoid Any
TYPE-005.description: Use object instead of Any for unknown types.
TYPE-005.ifThen: IF need unknown type THEN use object (or add # type: ignore comment)
TYPE-005.detectHeuristic: Any in type hints without comment

TYPE-006.name: Narrow parameters, wide returns
TYPE-006.description: Accept broad types (Iterable), return concrete types (list).
TYPE-006.ifThen: IF function accepts collection THEN use Iterable/Sequence; IF returns THEN use list/dict/tuple
TYPE-006.detectHeuristic: Parameter more specific than needed, return more abstract than actual

TYPE-007.name: Annotate all public signatures
TYPE-007.description: Every public function, method, and class attribute must have type annotations. Parameters, return types, and class __init__ -> None are all required.
TYPE-007.ifThen: IF function/method is public (no _ prefix) THEN annotate all parameters and return type
TYPE-007.detectHeuristic: Public function missing parameter or return type annotation
TYPE-007.correctPattern:
def get_user(user_id: str) -> User | None: ...
def __init__(self, db: Database) -> None:
